channel.title: MCP News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated news about Model Context Protocol, AI agents, tool integrations, MCP security, and developer tooling.

item.title: We Built Feedlog: A Free Feedback Tool for Startups (Looking for Early Beta Users)
item.description: As developers, we love building features. We ship fast, fix bugs, and continuously improve our products. But there's one problem that almost every side project, SaaS, or startup eventually runs into: User feedback is everywhere. A bug report comes through email. A feature request appears in...
item.dc:creator: Peggy

item.title: Turning a System of Record into an AI Agent: Building MCP Tools on Azure
item.description: A practical, end-to-end walkthrough of taking a read-only slice of an enterprise source system and exposing it to an AI agent as a set of Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools — using Azure Logic Apps, API Management, and Agent Foundry. All identifiers below are placeholders; swap in your own. The...
item.dc:creator: Vignesh Athiappan

item.title: AlgoVault MCP v1.23.2 — What's New
item.description: AlgoVault MCP v1.23.2 is live What's new: The default MCP tool set is 7 tools (trade calls, market scanner, market regime, funding arbitrage, and knowledge search/Q&A). MCP clients should refresh tools/list to update. Additional tools remain available to self-hosters behind opt-in environment flags...
item.dc:creator: AlgoVault.com

item.title: I built an MCP server to clean prompts — then learned MCP can't save your input tokens
item.description: I run a small tool that strips filler from LLM prompts — hedging, redundancy, pleasantries — while keeping every actual requirement. Paste a rambly 181-token prompt, get back the same request at 73 tokens. Same output from the model, 60% fewer input tokens. The obvious next step was: put this...
item.dc:creator: Vivek Chimman

item.title: How an AI agent posts Bitcoin as collateral without giving up the keys
item.description: Your AI agent just agreed to a trade that needs backing. A forward it has to deliver on in 24 hours. A loan it has to repay. A leg in a multi-step swap that only completes if every other leg does. Before any of that clears, the agent has to post collateral, and the deepest collateral asset in...
item.dc:creator: Baris Sozen

item.title: 61 Products, 92 Weekly Downloads, $7 Revenue: The Honest Numbers
item.description: The State of AgentPay Labs Three months ago I started building MCP servers full-time. 61 products later, here's the honest scorecard. What Exists 61 npm packages under @rumblingb 18 V12 finance shorts rendered and waiting 26 MCP server types across email verify, agent wallet, web scraping, data...
item.dc:creator: Rumblingb

item.title: 61 Products, 92 Weekly Downloads, $7 Revenue: The Honest Numbers
item.description: The State of AgentPay Labs Three months ago I started building MCP servers full-time. 61 products later, here's the honest scorecard. What Exists 61 npm packages under @rumblingb 18 V12 finance shorts rendered and waiting 26 MCP server types across email verify, agent wallet, web scraping, data...
item.dc:creator: Rumblingb

item.title: QR Code Generator API — 5분 안에 프로젝트에 통합하기
item.description: QR Code Generator API — 5분 안에 프로젝트에 통합하기 이커머스, 출석체크, 메뉴판, 결제링크 — QR 필요한 곳 어디든 프로젝트에 qr code generator 기능이 필요한데 직접 구현하기엔 시간이 아까운가요? REST API 한 줄이면 됩니다. Quick Start # 즉시 테스트 (API 키 불필요) curl https://api.lazy-mac.com/qr-code/ 실전 예제 Python import requests resp =...
item.dc:creator: 2x lazymac

item.title: AI agents need their own SSL. Here's why I built it.
item.description: In 1995, Netscape released SSL. The web didn't really take off commercially until then. Before SSL, you couldn't trust a website with your credit card. After SSL, e-commerce exploded. AI agents are at the same inflection point in 2026. Here's why. The problem Agents are starting to call each other...
item.dc:creator: Edison Flores

item.title: MCP Explained Simply: What It Is, Why It Matters, and How to Build Your First Server
item.description: I have worked in cloud and platform engineering for 16 years. When I first heard "agentic AI" and "MCP", it sounded complex. It is not. This post explains everything in simple words. You do not need to read anything else to understand MCP. There is a glossary at the bottom if any word is new to...
item.dc:creator: Jay Kumar

item.title: Post-mortem: a trojan slipped into my MCP marketplace. Here's what I built to stop it happening again.
item.description: Two weeks ago, a community member reported something alarming on my MCP marketplace: one of the skills contained a Windows trojan. This is the post-mortem. Not the "here's what went wrong" kind — the "here's what I built so it never happens again" kind. The incident Skill: prospector-email-finder...
item.dc:creator: Edison Flores

item.title: ATC is now real: Ed25519-signed agent trust cards with working verify + revoke
item.description: Yesterday I wrote about 8 security layers for my MCP marketplace. Today I'm shipping the 9th: Agent Trust Cards (ATC) — and this time the cryptography is real, not a mock. What is ATC? ATC = SSL certificates for AI agents. MarketNow acts as the Certificate Authority (CA). Every agent gets a...
item.dc:creator: Edison Flores

item.title: What's a Claude-compatible crypto trading research stack? (2026)
item.description: Intro A Claude-compatible crypto trading research stack in 2026 is Claude (the reasoning layer) plus the AlgoVault MCP (the decision layer that returns a composite verdict) plus a verifiable, on-chain record of past calls. Claude Code and Claude Desktop already speak the Model Context Protocol...
item.dc:creator: AlgoVault.com

item.title: Beyond text: Returning images and interactive apps from MCP servers
item.description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is becoming a richer foundation for agent experiences. Though most servers return plain text from their tool calls, MCP servers can also return binary results and provide interactive apps in clients that support those features, like VS Code. In this post, I'll use...
item.dc:creator: Pamela_Fox

item.title: Agent Runs now available in the Vercel MCP and CLI
item.description: Your agent can now inspect Agent Runs via the Vercel MCP and CLI for eve, the open-source agent framework.eve traces are automatically ingested when deployed to Vercel and available as Agent Runs. The new Vercel MCP tools and Vercel CLI commands let you find projects with runs, list recent runs,...
item.dc:creator: Brooke Mosby

item.title: Announcing the Monetization Gateway: charge for any resource behind Cloudflare via x402
item.description: We're opening the waitlist for our Monetization Gateway, which will allow you to charge for any web page, dataset, API, or MCP tool behind Cloudflare. The charges will settle in stablecoins over the x402 open protocol, with no payments stack of your own to build.
item.dc:creator: Will Papper

item.title: Beta SDKs for the 2026-07-28 MCP Spec Release Candidate Are Here
item.description: Beta releases of the Python, TypeScript, Go, and C# SDKs are now available with support for the 2026-07-28 MCP specification release candidate. Here is what changes for your server, how to migrate, and how to test before the spec goes final on July 28.
item.dc:creator: Model Context Protocol

item.title: MCP for Beginners: Why Every AI Engineer and Developer Should Learn the Model Context Protocol
item.description: If you have spent any time building with large language models in the last year, you have hit the same wall everyone hits: your model is brilliant at reasoning but blind to the real world. It cannot read your database, call your internal API, search your documents, or trigger a deployment unless...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: MCP Server Authorization with Azure API Management: From Simple to Advanced
item.description: Why put API Management in front of your MCP servers The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has quickly become the standard way for AI agents, such as GitHub Copilot in VS Code, to reach external tools and data. As soon as an MCP server does anything meaningful, the same questions that govern any API...
item.dc:creator: vzisiadis

item.title: Enterprise-Managed Authorization: Zero-touch OAuth for MCP
item.description: The Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension to the Model Context Protocol is now stable, enabling organizations to centrally provision MCP server access through their identity provider so users get connected servers on first login without per-app OAuth.
item.dc:creator: Model Context Protocol
